steps.title: Locate angle B and identify the sides
steps.content: The right angle is at vertex A (marked by the small square). Angle B is at the top right. From angle B, the adjacent side is BA = 5, and the hypotenuse is CB = 13.

steps.title: Apply the cosine ratio formula
steps.content:
In a right triangle, cosine equals adjacent over hypotenuse. Therefore: $$\cos B = \frac{5}{13}$$

Equivalent forms accepted: 5/13, 0.384, 0.385, 0.3846.
